clusters.neutral_headline: US-Saudi nuclear deal hinges on normalisation with Israel
bullet_summary: Meanwhile, Saudi Arabia has yet to respond after President Trump said the Saudis would have to join the Abraham Accords in order to finalize the U
President Trump said a proposed US-Saudi civilian nuclear deal is subject to Saudi Arabia joining the Abraham Accords
US President Donald Trump said Thursday that a nuclear deal with Saudi Arabia depends on the kingdom recognizing Israel -- throwing the agreement into turmoil just the day after it was signed
President Trump said on Thursday the deal would include no enrichment of nuclear material

items.title: Latest on U.S. military buildup in the Middle East; Saudi Arabia nuclear deal
items.standfirst: Sources say the United States military is building up its forces in the Middle East as President Trump warns that it will hold Iran accountable for any Houthi attacks. Meanwhile, Saudi Arabia has yet to respond after President Trump said the Saudis would have to join the Abraham Accords in order to finalize the U.S.-Saudi nuclear deal. CBS News' Kathryn Watson breaks it all down.
items.content: Sources say the United States military is building up its forces in the Middle East as President Trump warns that it will hold Iran accountable for any Houthi attacks. Meanwhile, Saudi Arabia has yet to respond after President Trump said the Saudis would have to join the Abraham Accords in order to finalize the U.S.-Saudi nuclear deal. CBS News' Kathryn Watson breaks it all down.

items.title: US-Saudi nuclear deal hinges on normalisation with Israel
items.standfirst: President Trump said a proposed US-Saudi civilian nuclear deal is subject to Saudi Arabia joining the Abraham Accords
items.content: President Trump said a proposed US-Saudi civilian nuclear deal is subject to Saudi Arabia joining the Abraham Accords

items.title: Trump stuns Saudis by pinning nuclear deal to Israel ties
items.standfirst: US President Donald Trump said Thursday that a nuclear deal with Saudi Arabia depends on the kingdom recognizing Israel -- throwing the agreement into turmoil just the day after it was signed. Riyadh has long resisted joining the so-called Abraham Accords with Israel, insisting especially after the Gaza conflict on a pathway to a Palestinian state. FRANCE 24's Emerald Maxwell reports.
items.content: US President Donald Trump said Thursday that a nuclear deal with Saudi Arabia depends on the kingdom recognizing Israel -- throwing the agreement into turmoil just the day after it was signed. Riyadh has long resisted joining the so-called Abraham Accords with Israel, insisting especially after the Gaza conflict on a pathway to a Palestinian state. FRANCE 24's Emerald Maxwell reports.

items.title: Saudis must recognise Israel for nuclear deal, says Trump
items.standfirst: The US president says the landmark agreement to help Saudis develop nuclear energy is dependent on it joining the Abraham Accords.
items.content: The US president says the landmark agreement to help Saudis develop nuclear energy is dependent on it joining the Abraham Accords.

items.title: Trump’s dubious nuclear deal with Saudi Arabia
items.standfirst: An accord lacking ‘gold standard’ safeguards on enrichment would carry proliferation risks
items.content: An accord lacking ‘gold standard’ safeguards on enrichment would carry proliferation risks

items.title: Trump says Saudi nuclear deal contingent on Riyadh normalising relations with Israel
items.standfirst: President Donald Trump on Thursday set a new condition to a US-Saudi nuclear deal signed the previous day by insisting the agreement was "totally subject to" the Gulf kingdom joining the Abraham Accords. Riyadh has resisted normalising relations with Israel, especially after the Gaza war, and the new condition throws the US-Saudi deal into turmoil.
items.content: President Donald Trump on Thursday set a new condition to a US-Saudi nuclear deal signed the previous day by insisting the agreement was "totally subject to" the Gulf kingdom joining the Abraham Accords. Riyadh has resisted normalising relations with Israel, especially after the Gaza war, and the new condition throws the US-Saudi deal into turmoil.

items.title: U.S. and Saudis sign nuclear deal, contingent on joining Abraham Accords
items.standfirst: President Trump said on Thursday the deal would include no enrichment of nuclear material.
items.content: President Trump said on Thursday the deal would include no enrichment of nuclear material.

items.title: Trump says Saudi Arabia must normalize Israel ties before nuclear deal is approved
items.standfirst: The president added that the deal “is totally subject to Saudi Arabia joining the very respected and successful Abraham Accords.”
items.content: The president added that the deal “is totally subject to Saudi Arabia joining the very respected and successful Abraham Accords.”

items.title: US signs nuclear deal with Saudi Arabia
items.standfirst: The US and Saudi Arabia have reached a nuclear cooperation deal.
items.content: The US and Saudi Arabia have reached a nuclear cooperation deal.

clusters.title: Trump to impose fresh wave of tariffs on more than 80 countries
clusters.neutral_headline: Trump to impose fresh wave of tariffs on more than 80 countries
bullet_summary: The White House said the duties target countries that have failed to curb imports made with forced labour
Reported by Guardian World, BBC Top Stories, Financial Times and others

items.title: Trump to impose fresh wave of tariffs on more than 80 countries
items.standfirst: New tariffs to replace 10% global duty set to expire on Friday as administration cites concerns over ‘forced labor’Donald Trump’s administration has imposed a fresh wave of sweeping tariffs on dozens of US trading partners to replace a 10% global duty that was set to expire early Friday morning – the latest attempt to instate aggressive trade policies despite challenges from the country’s highest court.The US will impose between a 10% or 12.5% tariff on more than 80 countries, which includes the United Kingdom, Mexico, Canada, Australia, India, China, and the 27 countries that make up the European Union. It effectively replaces the blanket 10% tariff that Trump imposed in February, right after the US supreme court declared that his many of his earlier tariffs were illegal. Continue reading...
items.content: New tariffs to replace 10% global duty set to expire on Friday as administration cites concerns over ‘forced labor’Donald Trump’s administration has imposed a fresh wave of sweeping tariffs on dozens of US trading partners to replace a 10% global duty that was set to expire early Friday morning – the latest attempt to instate aggressive trade policies despite challenges from the country’s highest court.The US will impose between a 10% or 12.5% tariff on more than 80 countries, which includes the United Kingdom, Mexico, Canada, Australia, India, China, and the 27 countries that make up the European Union. It effectively replaces the blanket 10% tariff that Trump imposed in February, right after the US supreme court declared that his many of his earlier tariffs were illegal. Continue reading...

items.title: US imposes tariffs on dozens of trade partners over 'forced labour' imports
items.standfirst: The tariffs target around 60 trading partners over claims they failed to properly stop forced labour.
items.content: The tariffs target around 60 trading partners over claims they failed to properly stop forced labour.

items.title: US hits 60 countries with new duties as Trump rebuilds tariff wall
items.standfirst: Administration pins measures on forced labour probe after Supreme Court struck down blanket levies
items.content: Administration pins measures on forced labour probe after Supreme Court struck down blanket levies

items.title: Trump imposes new tariffs on dozens of countries, citing forced labor concerns
items.standfirst: The Trump administration will impose tariffs of up to 12.5% on goods from 60 U.S. trading partners accused of failing to crack down on forced labor, extending the administration's tariffs on much of the world.
items.content: The Trump administration will impose tariffs of up to 12.5% on goods from 60 U.S. trading partners accused of failing to crack down on forced labor, extending the administration's tariffs on much of the world.

items.title: Trump to impose double-digit tariffs on dozens of countries
items.standfirst: The United States will slap taxes of 10% to 12.5% on imports from 60 countries accounting for 99% of U.S. imports, charging that they have inadequately enforced bans on goods produced by forced labor.
items.content: The United States will slap taxes of 10% to 12.5% on imports from 60 countries accounting for 99% of U.S. imports, charging that they have inadequately enforced bans on goods produced by forced labor.(Image credit: Mark Schiefelbein)

items.title: Trump announces double digit tariffs on 60 countries over forced labour concerns
items.standfirst: US President Donald Trump imposed new tariffs of up to 12.5% on imports from 60 countries on Thursday, replacing temporary levies that expire on Friday after the Supreme Court struck down his earlier trade measures. The White House said the duties target countries that have failed to curb imports made with forced labour.
items.content: US President Donald Trump imposed new tariffs of up to 12.5% on imports from 60 countries on Thursday, replacing temporary levies that expire on Friday after the Supreme Court struck down his earlier trade measures. The White House said the duties target countries that have failed to curb imports made with forced labour.

clusters.title: Indian activist ends 26-day hunger strike, though student protests continue
clusters.neutral_headline: India activist ends hunger strike after 26 days
bullet_summary: Reported by France24, BBC World, Al Jazeera

items.title: Indian activist ends 26-day hunger strike, though student protests continue
items.standfirst: Indian activist Sonam Wangchuk ended his 26-day hunger strike on Friday after talks with government ministers, but student-led protests over leaked medical entrance exam papers continued, with demonstrators demanding the education minister's resignation and wider reforms.
items.content: Indian activist Sonam Wangchuk ended his 26-day hunger strike on Friday after talks with government ministers, but student-led protests over leaked medical entrance exam papers continued, with demonstrators demanding the education minister's resignation and wider reforms.

items.title: India activist ends hunger strike after 26 days
items.standfirst: Sonam Wangchuk, a supporter of youth-led protesters demanding education reforms, says he wants to avert "possible violence".
items.content: Sonam Wangchuk, a supporter of youth-led protesters demanding education reforms, says he wants to avert "possible violence".

items.title: Indian activist Wangchuk ends 26-day hunger strike over exam paper leaks
items.standfirst: ⁠Sonam ⁠Wangchuk says he ended his fast to demand education minister's resignation 'in view of possible violence'.
items.content: ⁠Sonam ⁠Wangchuk says he ended his fast to demand education minister's resignation 'in view of possible violence'.

clusters.title: France evacuates 20,000 as wildfires spread in south-west

bullet_summary: Reported by BBC World, France24, Sky News World

items.title: France evacuates 20,000 as wildfires spread in south-west
items.standfirst: Most of those evacuated were tourists from campsites around Arcachon Bay in France's Gironde region.
items.content: Most of those evacuated were tourists from campsites around Arcachon Bay in France's Gironde region.

items.title: France : Wildfire sweeps through 2,000 hectares near tourist hotspot
items.standfirst: A wildfire is ripping through pine forests in the southwest of France, near the tourist hotspot of Arcachon. Nearly 12,000 people, including tourists staying at campgrounds, have been evacuated.
items.content: A wildfire is ripping through pine forests in the southwest of France, near the tourist hotspot of Arcachon. Nearly 12,000 people, including tourists staying at campgrounds, have been evacuated.

items.title: More than 15,000 people evacuated from homes as wildfire spreads in France
items.standfirst: Around 20,000 people were evacuated from towns and holiday resorts in southwestern France as a wildfire raged near the popular tourist coastal town of Lege Cap-Ferret.
items.content: Around 20,000 people were evacuated from towns and holiday resorts in southwestern France as a wildfire raged near the popular tourist coastal town of Lege Cap-Ferret.

clusters.title: New PM cuts business rates for UK pubs in latest cost-of-living measure
clusters.neutral_headline: New PM cuts business rates for UK pubs in latest cost-of-living measure

items.title: New PM cuts business rates for UK pubs in latest cost-of-living measure
items.standfirst: British Prime Minister Andy Burnham announced a 20% cut to business rates for pubs, clubs and live music venues in England on Thursday, his third major cost-of-living measure in three days as the new Labour leader seeks to revive the economy and support struggling high streets.
items.content: British Prime Minister Andy Burnham announced a 20% cut to business rates for pubs, clubs and live music venues in England on Thursday, his third major cost-of-living measure in three days as the new Labour leader seeks to revive the economy and support struggling high streets.

items.title: Business rates cut for pubs, clubs and music venues in England only ‘a small move’, say critics
items.standfirst: Hospitality businesses call on Andy Burnham to cut VAT and extend help to hotels and restaurantsPubs, clubs and live music venues in England will receive a 20% discount on their business rates bills as part of a £100m aid package but Andy Burnham has immediately faced calls to be more ambitious.The prime minister said the measure, aimed at supporting the high street and easing cost of living pressures, would be fully funded, including through a review of relief for businesses deemed not to make a positive contribution to communities, such as vape shops. Continue reading...
items.content: Hospitality businesses call on Andy Burnham to cut VAT and extend help to hotels and restaurantsPubs, clubs and live music venues in England will receive a 20% discount on their business rates bills as part of a £100m aid package but Andy Burnham has immediately faced calls to be more ambitious.The prime minister said the measure, aimed at supporting the high street and easing cost of living pressures, would be fully funded, including through a review of relief for businesses deemed not to make a positive contribution to communities, such as vape shops. clusters.title: GOP House Judiciary chair issues criminal referral against Jack Smith
clusters.neutral_headline: GOP House Judiciary chair issues criminal referral against Jack Smith
bullet_summary: Reported by CBS News, NPR News

items.title: GOP House Judiciary chair issues criminal referral against Jack Smith
items.standfirst: Former special counsel Jack Smith, who attempted to prosecute then-former President Trump for his actions after the 2020 election, may now become a target of the Justice Department. CBS News homeland security correspondent Nicole Sganga has the details.
items.content: Former special counsel Jack Smith, who attempted to prosecute then-former President Trump for his actions after the 2020 election, may now become a target of the Justice Department. CBS News homeland security correspondent Nicole Sganga has the details.

items.title: Republicans urge Justice Department to investigate former prosecutor Jack Smith
items.standfirst: The former special counsel led two criminal investigations of President Trump and has been a frequent target of Trump's ire on social media.
items.content: The former special counsel led two criminal investigations of President Trump and has been a frequent target of Trump's ire on social media.(Image credit: Saul Loeb)

clusters.title: UK ‘ready to defend itself’ after Iranian threat over US bases
clusters.neutral_headline: UK ‘ready to defend itself’ after Iranian threat over US bases
bullet_summary: Reported by Guardian World, BBC Top Stories

items.title: UK ‘ready to defend itself’ after Iranian threat over US bases
items.standfirst: IRGC claims base in south-west England was used by US to launch bombing missions, making it ‘legitimate target’Middle East crisis live – latest updatesThe government has said the UK is “ready to defend itself” after Iran’s military warned that any bases being used by the US were “legitimate targets”.The UK has allowed the US to launch “defensive” operations from British bases hosting American planes since the start of its war on Iran but has refused to help in offensive operations. Continue reading...
items.content: IRGC claims base in south-west England was used by US to launch bombing missions, making it ‘legitimate target’Middle East crisis live – latest updatesThe government has said the UK is “ready to defend itself” after Iran’s military warned that any bases being used by the US were “legitimate targets”.The UK has allowed the US to launch “defensive” operations from British bases hosting American planes since the start of its war on Iran but has refused to help in offensive operations. Continue reading...

items.title: UK 'ready to defend itself' after Iran says bases are 'legitimate targets'
items.standfirst: An Iranian military group has issued a warning to the UK over allowing US forces to use British bases for strikes on Iran.
items.content: An Iranian military group has issued a warning to the UK over allowing US forces to use British bases for strikes on Iran.

clusters.title: Tesla stock suffers double-digit drop on carmaker’s worst day in years
clusters.neutral_headline: Tesla stock suffers double-digit drop on carmaker’s worst day in years
bullet_summary: Reported by Guardian World, Guardian Business

items.title: Tesla stock suffers double-digit drop on carmaker’s worst day in years
items.standfirst: Elon Musk’s EV company lost a quarter of its value after revealing weak profits and high spending on AI and roboticsTesla’s stock fell about 13.5% on Thursday in one of the biggest single-day market losses in the company’s history.Elon Musk’s automaker has struggled throughout the year, losing about 27% of its market value as huge capital expenditures and weaker-than-expected profits worry investors. Continue reading...
items.content: Elon Musk’s EV company lost a quarter of its value after revealing weak profits and high spending on AI and roboticsTesla’s stock fell about 13.5% on Thursday in one of the biggest single-day market losses in the company’s history.Elon Musk’s automaker has struggled throughout the year, losing about 27% of its market value as huge capital expenditures and weaker-than-expected profits worry investors. Continue reading...

items.title: Tesla’s profits slide despite growing revenue as it pivots to robotics and AI
items.standfirst: Shares in Elon Musk company fall more 3% in after-hours trading, as earnings per share miss Wall Street expectationsTesla reported its second-quarter earnings on Wednesday, disclosing far lower profits than expected. The company’s already beleaguered stock, which had fallen about 14% this year to date, dipped further following the earnings report.Elon Musk’s automaker, once the pinnacle of his tech empire, has taken a back seat to SpaceX. Musk’s rocket and AI company held the largest stock market debut in history last month, turning the richest man on Earth into the world’s first trillionaire, though his net worth has since fallen from its peak. Continue reading...
items.content: Shares in Elon Musk company fall more 3% in after-hours trading, as earnings per share miss Wall Street expectationsTesla reported its second-quarter earnings on Wednesday, disclosing far lower profits than expected. The company’s already beleaguered stock, which had fallen about 14% this year to date, dipped further following the earnings report.Elon Musk’s automaker, once the pinnacle of his tech empire, has taken a back seat to SpaceX. Musk’s rocket and AI company held the largest stock market debut in history last month, turning the richest man on Earth into the world’s first trillionaire, though his net worth has since fallen from its peak. items.title: DOJ to withdraw subpoenas for New York Times reporters over Air Force One story
items.standfirst: The Justice Department withdrew its subpoenas for New York Times reporters during a court heraing Thursday, after a judge grilled government lawyers for making "errors."
items.content: The Justice Department withdrew its subpoenas for New York Times reporters during a court heraing Thursday, after a judge grilled government lawyers for making "errors."

items.title: US government cancels subpoena against three New York Times reporters
items.standfirst: Withdrawal is latest instance of government's efforts to crack down on critical media facing setbacks in court.
items.content: Withdrawal is latest instance of government's efforts to crack down on critical media facing setbacks in court.

clusters.title: Reinstating Fedorov 'only right decision Zelensky can make', MP says
clusters.neutral_headline: Ukraine's ousted defence minister insists on being reinstated

items.title: Reinstating Fedorov 'only right decision Zelensky can make', MP says
items.standfirst: Ousted Ukrainian defence minister Mykhailo Fedorov said on Thursday he would ​accept no other government position besides his former job, escalating a political crisis that has gripped Ukraine at a pivotal point in the war with Russia. Speaking with FRANCE 24's Mark Owen, Ukranian MP Liudmyla Buimister says that reinstating Fedorov "is the only right decision that President Zelensky can make".
items.content: Ousted Ukrainian defence minister Mykhailo Fedorov said on Thursday he would ​accept no other government position besides his former job, escalating a political crisis that has gripped Ukraine at a pivotal point in the war with Russia. Speaking with FRANCE 24's Mark Owen, Ukranian MP Liudmyla Buimister says that reinstating Fedorov "is the only right decision that President Zelensky can make".

items.title: Ukraine's ousted defence minister insists on being reinstated
items.standfirst: Mykhailo Fedorov refuses President Zelensky's offer to accept an alternative post, as the battle of wills continues.
items.content: Mykhailo Fedorov refuses President Zelensky's offer to accept an alternative post, as the battle of wills continues.
